title: betula ermanii f. corticosa
intro: Betula ermanii f. corticosa, also known as Korean paper birch or Manchurian birch, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its white, papery bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of this plant are attributed to its betulin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Betula ermanii f. corticosa is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting digestion. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine in Asia, particularly in China, Korea, and Japan.
